From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Filk music award

This article is about the filk music award. For the French award for video games, see [Pégases](/wiki/P%C3%A9gases "Pégases"). For the satirical award that seeks to expose parapsychological frauds, see [Pigasus Award](/wiki/Pigasus_Award "Pigasus Award").

[![Photograph of several people on stage in front of a black curtain bearing the letters O V F F.](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/20/OVFF_2012_-_Mary_Crowell_and_Friends_%288156652529%29.jpg/250px-OVFF_2012_-_Mary_Crowell_and_Friends_%288156652529%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:OVFF_2012_-_Mary_Crowell_and_Friends_(8156652529).jpg)

Mary Crowell and others on stage at Ohio Valley Filk Fest 2012

The **Pegasus Award** is the premier award for [filk music](/wiki/Filk_music "Filk music")[[1]](#cite_note-decatur-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3) and is annually hosted at the Ohio Valley Filk Fest (OVFF).

## Awards

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Pegasus_Award&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Awards")]

The Pegasus Awards were founded to recognize and honor excellence in filking. As [science fiction](/wiki/Science_fiction "Science fiction") (sci-fi) became better known and widespread within society in the 20th century as a distinct literary genre,[[4]](#cite_note-4) many fans expressed themselves through works of music and art, including filking. The Pegasus Awards formally recognise filking or fan music as a fine lens that the sci-fi community shares with particular fiction-based cultural ideas.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

Anyone with an interest in filk can nominate songs or individuals for the awards, and anyone can vote. It is not necessary to be a member of the convention to be involved in the nomination and voting process.

Currently awards are given in six categories: Best Song, Best Performer, Best Writer/Composer, Best Classic Song and two topical categories that vary from year to year. Some examples of past categories include: Best Love Song, Best Literature Song, Best Techie Song, Best Sing Along, etc.

The OVFF convention committee solicits nominations for finalists for the Pegasus Awards (the Nominating Ballot) during the late spring and summer. There is an opinion poll that runs during the year as well to help interested folk brainstorm ideas for the nominating ballot. The finalist ballot is distributed in the early fall, and must be returned by the opening night of OVFF. Voting can be done online—either to nominate finalists, or to vote for the finalists themselves. The final round of voting happens at OVFF itself, where handwritten ballots are collected after the annual Pegasus concert. The entire process is administered by the OVFF convention committee.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

## Ohio Valley Filk Fest

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Pegasus_Award&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Ohio Valley Filk Fest")]

| Ohio Valley Filk Fest | |
| --- | --- |
| Status | Active |
| Genre | [Filk music](/wiki/Filk_music "Filk music") |
| Location | [Worthington, Ohio](/wiki/Worthington,_Ohio "Worthington, Ohio") |
| Country | United States |
| Inaugurated | 1984 |
| Website | <http://www.ovff.org/> |

The Ohio Valley Filk Fest, (OVFF) is one of the largest [filk music](/wiki/Filk_music "Filk music") [conventions](/wiki/Science_fiction_conventions "Science fiction conventions").[[7]](#cite_note-7) It is held annually in October in the [Columbus, Ohio](/wiki/Columbus,_Ohio "Columbus, Ohio") area, and the culmination of the event is the presentation of the Pegasus Award.[[8]](#cite_note-8)

The first OVFF was held in 1984 in [Cincinnati](/wiki/Cincinnati,_Ohio "Cincinnati, Ohio") and was attended by about 80 people; it included the presentation of the first Pegasus Awards. After skipping 1985, it moved to the [Columbus](/wiki/Columbus,_Ohio "Columbus, Ohio") area but retained "Ohio Valley" in its name. The convention has been held every year since then, in various locations in Columbus. Recent conventions had been held in [Dublin, Ohio](/wiki/Dublin,_Ohio "Dublin, Ohio"), but as of 2010 the convention has moved to [Worthington](/wiki/Worthington,_Ohio "Worthington, Ohio").
